steps.title: Identify the central contrast
steps.content: The passage establishes a key contrast: the building's impracticality (designed to be “more fanciful than functional”) produces tangible health benefits, as shown by Yamaoka's “significant health benefits” after four years.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: Choice D directly expresses this central claim—an impractical design can improve well-being. Choices A, B, and C either introduce unsupported claims or mischaracterize the evidence. That matches choice D — D is correct.
